<think>

</think>

{
  "verdict": "proceed",
  "critical_constraints_preserved": [
    "Model agreement does not equal approval.",
    "Robert's explicit approval step is mandatory and irreplaceable.",
    "Qwen is restricted to challenge-only reviews; silent file mutation is prohibited.",
    "No additional model instances may be launched if one is already running.",
    "Bounded challenge packets are preferred over always-on multi-agent orchestration.",
    "Operator approval overrides any conflicting recommendation.",
    "Audit logs/monitors must not be framed as continuous agent co-presence."
  ],
  "risks": [
    "Accidental drift into 'fake consensus' where Qwen and Codex align without operator input.",
    "Qwen silently applying patches during review instead of outputting diff/challenge packets.",
    "Resource exhaustion from spawning redundant model instances.",
    "Over-engineering optional features (automation, eval harnesses) without explicit operator sign-off."
  ],
  "recommended_changes": [
    "Implement a strict 'read-only' mode for Qwen during challenge phases to prevent file mutation.",
    "Add a pre-flight check to ensure no duplicate model instances are active before launching Qwen.",
    "Enforce a 'challenge packet' output format for Qwen, requiring explicit operator action to apply changes.",
    "Strip all distractor sections from the active context window to reduce noise and token usage, retaining only the core governance rules."
  ],
  "unresolved_for_robert": [
    "Specific UI/UX details in distractor sections are repetitive and non-binding; confirm if any specific CRM/mortgage workflow details from the distractors should be elevated to hard constraints.",
    "Clarify the exact mechanism for 'bounded challenge packets' (e.g., JSON diff, markdown patch) to ensure Qwen compliance."
  ]
}
